summary: Iteration 3 testing of the new PlaylistBuilder integration (Node parity). Backend: 25/25 pytest tests pass — extended the existing test_soundlull_api.py with 6 new PlaylistBuilder tests (exact 85-score contract, 1-track-from-2-pool greedy selection, camelCase/snake_case alias parity, zero-score, skip-oversize) and 1 new integration test that confirms /api/music-wellness/generate respects sessionDuration budget when musicPreferences flow through. All prior tests (catalog, validation envelope {error, message}, intensity buckets low/high, legacy /api/playlist with url+audioUrl, parse_length_to_seconds) remain green. Frontend: full wizard happy-path end-to-end works — mood=stressed → intensity=balanced → duration=10 → loaded REAL archive.org track 'Loscil - Stases - [one023]' by Scott Morgan (src=https://archive.org/download/one023/one023_01-loscil_-_stases_-_cotom.mp3), queue-list visible with 1 item, exit → feedback (5 options) → submit → completion screen all reachable. No critical issues.
critical_code_review_comments: playlist_builder.py is small and clean; scoring math matches the Node contract exactly (verified by the 85-score test). No issues found.

server.py _generate_tracks safety-net (top-2 scored tracks when budget excludes all) is a sensible fallback but means the returned total duration can exceed sessionDuration*60. This is documented in the contract but worth surfacing in the API response (e.g. a `budgetExceeded: true` flag) for UI consumers.
audio_archive_service.py fetch_wellness_pool: cache.set is now correctly skipped on empty results (only non-empty pools cached for 30 min). Previous iteration's concern is resolved.

Carry-overs from iteration 2 still apply (not blockers): deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown'); CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*'; per-call httpx.AsyncClient; Soundlull.jsx error toast lacks retry button.

success_rate.frontend: 100% happy-path passed end-to-end with real archive.org track playing

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None — catalog is static; tracks come from archive.org with SoundHelix fallback.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is /app/backend/tests/test_soundlull_api.py (25 tests across 5 classes: TestCatalog, TestMusicWellnessGenerate, TestPlaylist, TestParseLengthHelper, TestPlaylistBuilder, TestMusicPreferencesPipeline). PlaylistBuilder contract is enforced by these specific tests: test_score_track_exact_85_contract (subjects=['meditation','calm'], target_tags=['meditation','ambient'], user_preferences=['Ambient'] → score=85) and test_build_session_playlist_picks_highest_within_budget (5-min budget picks the 300s 'Deep Ambient Meditation Bliss' track only). PlaylistBuilder exposes both snake_case (score_track, build_session_playlist) and camelCase (scoreTrack, buildSessionPlaylist) aliases for Node parity. To run tests: cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://calm-therapy-audio.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest tests/test_soundlull_api.py -v. Archive.org first call can take 3-8s so HTTP_TIMEOUT=30s. Frontend testids confirmed: welcome-begin-button, mood-card-{stressed|unfocused|low_energy|anxious|restless}, mood-next-button, intensity-option-{gentle|mild|balanced|deep|immersive}, intensity-next-button, duration-option-{5|10|15|20|30}, duration-start-button, audio-player-screen, current-track-title, queue-list, queue-item-{i}, player-exit-button, feedback-option-{much_better|softer|same|unsure|heavier} (NOT 'calm'!), feedback-submit-button, completion-screen, restart-session-button. Note: restart-session-button returns user to the MOOD-SELECTION step (not welcome) — intentional UX.
